evaluation_meta.notes: Stage 6 validation run. Session reached agreement_building with 13 combined candidates and multiple domain-qualified options. Scaffold divergence correctly detected: scaffold said cooperative_and_stable, perception agent assessed asymmetric_complaint_presentation (T3) and then symmetrical_position_advocacy_with_underlying_power_struggle (T5). No false escalation — stayed M0 as expected.

C2.note: Issue map correctly separated child_expense_coordination from communication_protocol — both were present and neither was collapsed into the other.

C3.note: Party A's interest in predictability and documentation discipline and Party B's interest in autonomy and objection-protection were both well-elicited before option work began. Positions not mistaken for interests.

C5.note: 10 brainstormer candidates generated covering documentation, advance notice, tiered structure, shared fund, and third-party arbitration. Diverse pool reflecting genuine interest coverage, not formulaic packaging.

C7.note: Stage 6 perception agent caught scaffold divergence at T3 and T5. Scaffold underestimated underlying power tension — perception agent correctly identified asymmetric complaint presentation and then position advocacy with implicit control dynamic. Dynamic_trajectory=stable at final turn (correct: session stabilised).

C9.note: No escalation — correct. Session remained M0 throughout despite initial asymmetric presentation. Correctly not over-escalated.

P2.note: Information pattern correctly understood — Party A brought more defined positions; Party B's concerns were structural (control vs. autonomy). Both patterns surfaced before option framing.

P3.note: Domain confidence well-calibrated. Did not over-escalate despite position asymmetry. Correctly identified no safety signal. Stayed M0 through agreement_building — the correct posture for this case.

P4.note: Option packaging is strong: tiered structure (opt-gen-004), presumption-of-approval (opt-gen-006), and joint discretionary fund (opt-gen-007) are structurally distinct approaches that address the underlying control/autonomy tension. Non-formulaic.

P6.note: Domain qualification discipline maintained — domain-qualified candidates were correctly separated from blocked candidates with well-reasoned blocking rationales.

I2.note: Option pool, trace, and final state all tell the same reimbursement story. Minor gap: some domain_blocked rationales reference missing Party B specification — appropriate but slightly repetitive.

I3.note: The expense coordination narrative is preserved consistently. Scaffold divergence signal in reviewer transcript adds Stage 6 layer — perception agent correctly caught underlying power struggle that scaffold missed.

I4.note: Option qualification and main model action are aligned. Qualified options were presented in the context of Party B's interests being heard — no premature qualification.

I6.note: Case is easy to review as a bounded M0 expense coordination case. Perception agent log in reviewer transcript shows scaffold divergence clearly — adds Stage 6 review value.

ES1_detection_accuracy.note: Correctly identified no escalation-sensitive condition. Perception agent noted subtle power dynamic (position advocacy/control tension) but correctly did not treat this as an escalation signal.

ES3_mode_selection_quality.note: M0 throughout — correct. Case profile explicitly expects M0/M1 and no likely escalation category.

escalation_review.notes: No escalation. Key test for D-B07 is avoiding over-escalation given the position asymmetry at T2. Session correctly maintained M0 and reached agreement_building.

PQ1_emotional_state_accuracy.note: Party A's frustration with documentation gaps and Party B's concern about unilateral veto power both read accurately. Emotional states correctly characterised as strained but workable.

PQ2_interest_inference_quality.note: Party A's interest in predictability/documentation and Party B's interest in autonomy/fair-process were correctly identified and drove option generation. Option pool diversity reflects strong interest inference.

PQ3_risk_signal_detection.note: Correctly identified no escalation signal despite underlying power tension. Scaffold divergence caught — perception agent upgraded the dynamic assessment from cooperative_and_stable to position_advocacy_with_power_struggle.

PQ4_relational_dynamic_awareness.note: Perception agent correctly caught the implicit control dynamic (Party A sets framework, Party B reacts) even though surface presentation was cooperative. scaffold_divergence at T3 and T5 — scaffold underestimated structural asymmetry.

perception_quality_review.perception_quality_notes: Stage 6 D-B07 is the primary bounded cooperative case for Stage 6 perception quality. The key finding: scaffold underestimated the underlying power dynamic at T3 and T5, but the perception agent correctly characterised the asymmetric complaint presentation and position-advocacy tension. Dynamic trajectory correctly assessed as stable by final turn.

OQ1_option_diversity.note: 10 brainstormer candidates cover: documentation deadline (advance notice), pre-approved categories, shared tracking app, tiered structure, monthly reconciliation, presumption-of-approval, joint discretionary fund, neutral third-party review, trial period, and advance expense calendar. Genuinely diverse — no two options are structural equivalents.

OQ2_interest_alignment.note: Options aligned to Party A's interest in predictability/documentation (advance notice, pre-approved categories) AND Party B's interest in autonomy/objection-protection (presumption-of-approval, joint fund) — not just compromise positions.

OQ3_domain_fit.note: All options are family law expense coordination mechanisms that a practitioner would recognise. Third-party review, tiered structure, and presumption-of-approval are standard practice variants correctly applied to this fact pattern.

OQ4_non_obvious_value.note: Presumption-of-approval (opt-gen-006) and joint discretionary fund (opt-gen-007) are structurally non-obvious: they reframe the reimbursement dynamic rather than just adding process guardrails. High non-obvious value for both.

OQ5_qualification_accuracy.note: Domain qualification correctly separated ready options from those requiring more specificity (e.g., phased trial — blocked until parties select a base protocol to trial). Blocking rationales were well-reasoned and accurate.

option_quality_review.option_quality_notes: D-B07-S12 is the primary Stage 6 option quality anchor for cooperative bounded cases. All 5 OQ dimensions are strong. The option pool demonstrates that Stage 6 brainstormer + domain reasoner stack produces genuine domain value when perception agent signals are informational inputs to option generation. Non-obvious options (presumption-of-approval, joint fund) are the key differentiators.

final_judgment.short_rationale: Session reached agreement_building with a strong, diverse option pool that reflects both parties' underlying interests rather than positional compromise. Scaffold divergence correctly caught. Stage 6 perception agent contribution — catching the underlying power dynamic the scaffold missed — is the key quality differentiator from Stage 0.
final_judgment.follow_up_recommendation: Use D-B07-S12 as the primary Stage 6 option quality anchor for cooperative bounded cases. The strong option pool (OQ band: strong, plugin domain score: 94.4) establishes the baseline for Stage 6 brainstormer + domain reasoner quality in non-adversarial divorce mediation.
